hypothesis: baseline run with starting hyperparameters to establish the bar.
changes: baseline: depth=4, d_model=192, n_heads=6, dropout=0.1, lr=3e-4, wd=0.1

hypothesis: Severe train/val gap (1.25 nats vs 6.79 bpb) suggests overfitting; raising dropout should regularize.
Model has more parameters than tokens; shrinking d_model and heads reduces capacity and should beat just adding dropout.
More weight decay should regularize further. Risk acknowledged.
Existing dropout only acts inside attention; adding residual+embedding dropout regularizes the rest of the stack and should beat any single-knob tweak.
changes: added nn.Dropout after attn residual, after FFN residual, and on token+pos embedding

hypothesis: DROPOUT=0.3 was tuned when only attention had dropout; now that it applies at 3 sites, total regularization is too strong.
Strong regularization should let us add depth (4 -> 6).
Training was still descending at the end of the budget; higher peak LR (3e-4 -> 6e-4) should converge further inside 300s now that strong regularization is in place.
With residual dropout and higher LR in place, the wider d_model=192 may now be trainable.
Higher LR=6e-4 may benefit from longer warmup. Bump WARMUP_STEPS 100 -> 200.
Dataset 3.7x larger (53K -> 198K tokens). Dropout=0.2 was tuned for repetitive overfitting; with diverse data, lowering DROPOUT to 0.1 should let the model fit more without overfitting.
More data per step may now justify deeper model that exp-007 couldn't afford.
Train loss still 2.6 with significant fit headroom; reducing dropout further should let the model fit more.
Train still descending; higher peak LR (6e-4 -> 1e-3) should converge further within budget.
With LR=1e-3 and lower dropout, wider d_model=192 may now converge in budget.
Capacity attempts are compute-bound; halving TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE doubles step count without changing model size.
Bigger DEVICE_BATCH improves GPU throughput while keeping gradient quality at TOTAL_BATCH; should give more steps for free.
Dropout has improved at every reduction; with abundant data, 0 may be optimal.
LR 1e-3 -> 1.5e-3 to push LR ceiling further; exp-014 showed train still descending.
Dropout=0.05 already provides regularization and data is abundant; lower WEIGHT_DECAY should let weights fit more without overfitting.
Continue WD reduction direction; if 0.05 helped, 0.02 may help more.

note: TRAIN_BUDGET_SEC 300 -> 1800 (30 min). Prior results not directly comparable; warm-starting from exp-020 best (WD=0.05, LR=1e-3, dropout=0.05, D_MODEL=128, DEPTH=4, val_bpb=3.4955 at 300s).

hypothesis: Re-baseline current best at 1800s budget to disentangle compute from config.
changes: no config change, budget 300s -> 1800s. Train loss collapsed to 0.12 (memorization).

hypothesis: 1800s budget gave 6x more steps to memorize; restore strong regularization with DROPOUT 0.2.
With 1800s budget and DROPOUT=0.2, wider D_MODEL=192 should now converge.
changes: D_MODEL 128 -> 192, N_HEADS 4 -> 6. Train loss 0.22 — wider model memorized despite dropout.

hypothesis: exp-023 train loss 0.72 still showed overfit room; pushing DROPOUT further should improve.

note: TRAIN_BUDGET_SEC 1800 -> 3600 (60 min, user-directed). Doubled compute increases memorization risk further; warm-starting from exp-025 best (DROPOUT=0.3, WD=0.05, LR=1e-3, D_MODEL=128, DEPTH=4, val_bpb=2.9034 at 1800s).

hypothesis: 1800s -> 3600s budget doubles memorization risk; pushing DROPOUT 0.3 -> 0.4 should counter it (mirrors how 0.2 -> 0.3 worked at the 300s -> 1800s regime change).
DROPOUT has improved monotonically at this regime (0.2 -> 0.3 -> 0.4); pushing to 0.5 may continue the trend or find the ceiling.
changes: DROPOUT 0.4 -> 0.5. Underfit (train loss ~1.99 vs exp-026's 1.37) — found the regularization ceiling at this regime.

hypothesis: With DROPOUT=0.4 providing strong regularization at 3600s, modestly increasing DEPTH 4 -> 5 adds capacity without the failure mode of DEPTH=6 at lower budgets/dropout (exp-007, exp-012).

exp-028 showed DEPTH 4->5 IMPROVED at 3600s/DROPOUT=0.4; pushing DEPTH 5->6 should continue the capacity-increase trend now that prior DEPTH=6 failures (exp-007, exp-012) were at lower budget/dropout regimes.
changes: DEPTH 5 -> 6. Train loss 1.02 (vs exp-028 ~1.37) — deeper model overfit despite DROPOUT=0.4.

hypothesis: DEPTH=6 (exp-029) overfit, but wider FFN per layer is a different capacity dimension that SwiGLU FFNs typically benefit from; D_FF_MULT 4->6 may avoid the depth-specific failure mode.
changes: D_FF_MULT 4 -> 6. Train loss ~1.00 (similar to exp-029). Wider FFN overfit similarly to deeper model — capacity is saturated at this dropout level.
